channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: FBI investigating claims of Comey-era 'honeypot' operation against Trump 2016 campaign: report
item.description: The Federal Bureau of Investigation is reportedly investigating whistleblower claims of a 2015 "honeypot" operation against the 2016 Trump presidential campaign.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Eric Adams lawyers seek to dismiss charges with prejudice
item.description: Attorneys for New York City Mayor Eric Adams are seeking to have all charges against their client dropped after the Trump administration moved to drop the corruption case.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Canada wants new oil pipelines to avoid Trump tariffs; nobody wants to build them
item.description: Canada sends 90 percent of oil exports to U.S. refiners. The Canadian government would have to play a significant role in any project to build new oil pipelines in Canada to overcome regulatory, financial and political hurdles and activist opposition, industry experts said.
item.dc:creator: MPR News

item.title: Ratcliffe shrugs off concerns about potential threat of fired agents armed with CIA's secrets
item.description: If firing CIA officers would prompt them to "risk treason" by spilling nation's secrets, they need to part with the agency, which is the thinking of Director John Ratcliffe.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel and Hamas agree on a new exchange, leaving a fragile ceasefire intact
item.description: The bodies of four Israeli hostages are to be returned late Wednesday and more than 600 Palestinian prisoners and detainees freed during the last week of the current Gaza ceasefire deal.
item.dc:creator: Daniel Estrin and Hadeel Al-Shalchi, MPR News

item.title: Bexar County hires veteran election official pushed out of previous job amid right-wing criticism
item.description: Michele Carew was unanimously appointed as election administrator for the county that’s home to San Antonio, four years after leaving her post in Hood County.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Natalia Contreras, Votebeat And The Texas Tribune, KPRC 2

item.title: How the Supreme Court of the United States and federal courts work and affect Texans
item.description: Here’s how federal courts help determine the fate of controversial issues, such as immigration laws, in Texas and beyond.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By María Méndez, Graphics By Yuriko Schumacher, KPRC 2

item.title: Dolton, Illinois, mayor Tiffany Henyard projected to lose primary in landside
item.description: Dolton, Illinois, mayor Tiffany Henyard, a Democrat, is projected to lose in a landslide in the village's mayoral primary held on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Man who was mad about Chinese spy balloon faces sentencing for threatening ex-Speaker McCarthy
item.description: A Montana man faces years in federal prison at his sentencing on Wednesday for threatening to assault former House Speaker Kevin McCarthy.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Brown, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Latvia FM: Putin will try to achieve in peace talks what he couldn't in Ukraine war
item.description: Latvia’s foreign minister is warning that Russian President Vladimir Putin will try to achieve in peace talks what he hasn’t been able to accomplish in his war against Ukraine — weaken the United States and restore control over his smaller neighbor.
item.dc:creator: Edith M. Lederer, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Judge OKs prison abuse settlement, rejecting Trump administration's push to rewrite protections
item.description: A judge has given final approval to a legal settlement that subjects the federal Bureau of Prisons to independent monitoring and increased transparency in the wake of rampant staff-on-inmate sexual abuse at a now-shuttered federal women’s prison in California.
item.dc:creator: Michael R. Sisak, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Judge gives Trump administration two days to release billions of dollars in blocked foreign aid
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal judge on Tuesday gave the Trump administration less than two days to release billions of dollars in U.S. foreign aid, saying the administration had given no sign of complying with his nearly two-week-old court order to ease its funding freeze.
item.dc:creator: ELLEN KNICKMEYER and MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump administration creates registry for immigrants who are in the US illegally
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration is creating a registry for all people who are in the United States illegally, and those who don't self-report could face fines or prosecution, immigration officials announced Tuesday. Everyone who is in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA SANTANA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Noem announces DHS registry for illegal aliens in US that will require them to be fingerprinted
item.description: Department of Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem told illegal immigrants to either self-deport, register and be fingerprinted with the federal government or jeopardize their chance of freedom.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: House GOP pushes 'big' budget resolution to passage, a crucial step toward delivering Trump's agenda
item.description: With a push from President Donald Trump, House Republicans sent a GOP budget blueprint to passage Tuesday, a step toward delivering his “big, beautiful bill” with $4.5 trillion in tax breaks and $2 trillion in spending cuts despite a wall of opposition from Democrats and discomfort among Republicans
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: AG Pam Bondi faces pressure to release Epstein, JFK files
item.description: Rep. Anna Paulina Luna (R-Fla.) similarly requested the release of the Epstein files in a Monday post on X.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: 21 DOGE staffers resign, saying they won’t help ‘dismantle’ public services
item.description: Twenty-one members of the United States DOGE Service have resigned, they said in an anonymous letter, citing DOGE’s ongoing work dramatically reshaping the federal government.
item.dc:creator: Bobby Allyn, MPR News

item.title: Social Security says higher payments are on the way for millions of former public workers
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — More than 3.2 million Social Security recipients who received pensions from their time as teachers, firefighters, police officers and other public service jobs will soon see a boost in their benefits.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump directs government to consider possible tariffs on copper
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump on Tuesday directed the government to consider possible tariffs on copper, the latest move by the White House to tax a wide array of imports and reshape global trade.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Prosecutors conclude Army veteran's Capitol riot pardon also covers a separate weapons case
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Justice Department has concluded that a military veteran's presidential pardon for charges that he stormed the U.S. Capitol also extends to his separate conviction for illegally possessing stolen grenades and classified information, according to a court filing Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Zelensky expected to visit White House amid push for minerals deal
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky is expected to visit the White House on Friday as Kyiv and the Trump administration work to finalize an agreement on critical minerals.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Student loan borrowers face abrupt change as GOP budget plans threaten to raise payments
item.description: Student loan borrowers could see their payments increase under budget plans currently under discussion by House Republicans, a violent about-face after years of working with the Biden administration to lower payments and secure debt relief.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5

item.title: UNLV testing cameras to read license plates in aftermath of deadly 2023 attack
item.description: Cameras that can read license plates are part of the security response that followed the Dec. 5, 2023, shooting on the UNLV campus. They are just part of what UNLV is doing in an effort to keep students safer.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: US Marshals Service deputizes Musk's security team: Reports
item.description: The U.S. Marshals Service has deputized some of tech billionaire Elon Musk's private security detail, giving them specific rights and protections of federal law enforcement agents, multiple outlets reported.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, Fox 5

item.title: Florida GOP pushes for government efficiency with DOGE-inspired legislation
item.description: President Donald Trump's Department of Government Efficiency, also known as DOGE, has stirred up controversy as it searches for wasteful federal spending. Now, Florida GOP lawmakers are planning a similar initiative.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC
